LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_SalvageTrait_desc: Removes the given upgrade from this weapon. It can be reapplied to another unique weapon.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_AddTrait_desc: Adds a previously salvaged upgrade to this weapon. If you want to replace an existing upgrade, remove it first.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_WeaponUpgradeNotAvail: No upgrades available for this weapon
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddler_WeaponUpgrade_Desc: This is a weapon upgrade '{TRAIT_label}', its category is {CATEGORY}. \nA weapon upgrade allows you to customize unique weapons, adding new features or removing pain points. Applying a weapon upgrade is reversible; you can always remove it later. \nThe main source of upgrades is salvaged weapons from bygone eras.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_requiresMachi: Applying or salvaging weapon upgrades requires Machining research to be completed. Default: Yes.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_weaponTraitMa: Maximum amount of unique weapon upgrades: {COUNT}
When adding new weapon upgrades, the player can add up to this many upgrades. This does not affect unique weapons generation. Default is 3.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_personaWeapon: Persona weapons can have upgrades salvaged and applied. Default: Yes.
Applying or salvaging weapon upgrades from persona weapons requires Machine Persuasion research to be completed. Note: This stacks with the required Machining research option. Default: Yes.
Maximum amount of persona weapon upgrades: {COUNT}
When adding new weapon upgrades, the player can add up to this many upgrades. This does not affect persona weapons generation. Default is 2.
LanguageData.WeaponTraitFiddlerSettings_weaponCanBeDa: Weapon can be damaged by unskilled fiddling
Pawns with low skill have a chance to damage a weapon when applying or salvaging an upgrade. Default: Yes.
